title: backprop-trace canonical receipt schema v0.4.0
description: v0.6 external trace ingestion. Additive over v0.3.0: adds top-level source_framework + attestor blocks for observer-mode receipts (foreign frameworks computed the math; backprop-trace engine re-ran it as differential witness), extends fixture_status enums with external_imported authoring + engine_recompute_* verification values. v0.3.0 softmax+CE receipts continue to validate against v0.3.0 schema unchanged.
x-changes-from-v0.3.0: NEW optional top-level source_framework: when authoring_state === 'external_imported', describes which framework produced the math (name, version, optional extractor identifying the adapter that wrote the sidecar)

NEW optional top-level attestor: when authoring_state === 'external_imported', carries computed_by (the framework) + verified_by (the backprop-trace engine) + differential_tolerance (looser than engine-authored receipts because foreign FP precision drifts) + optional import_provenance (source hash + timestamp) + optional skip_basis (closed enum for engine_recompute_skipped_with_basis verification state) + optional signed_subject_digest (Rule 16 gating)

fixture_status.authoring_state enum widened: existing [hand_derived, engine_generated, engine_generated_general, deliberately_corrupted] + 'external_imported' (foreign framework produced the math; backprop-trace ingested via bp import)

fixture_status.verification_state enum widened: existing [pending_engine_reproduction, engine_reproduced_byte_equal, expected_to_fail_reconciliation] + three external states: 'engine_recompute_matched_within_tolerance' (Rule 14 differential check passed), 'engine_recompute_disagreed' (Rule 14 fired; receipt retained for audit), 'engine_recompute_skipped_with_basis' (recompute deliberately skipped; attestor.skip_basis names the reason)

NO removed fields. NO renamed fields. NO breaking changes to forward / loss / backward / updates / parameters_after / post_update_forward / post_update_loss — those are byte-identical shape to v0.3.0. v0.3.0 softmax+CE receipts validate against v0.3.0 schema unchanged; v0.6 observer-mode receipts declare schema_version: '0.4.0'.

source_framework.description: OPTIONAL. REQUIRED at the reconciler boundary (Rule 0 sub-check) when fixture_status.authoring_state === 'external_imported'. Describes which framework computed the math. Engine-authored receipts (authoring_state in {engine_generated, engine_generated_general, hand_derived}) MUST omit this block.

attestor.description: OPTIONAL. REQUIRED at the reconciler boundary when fixture_status.authoring_state === 'external_imported'. Carries the verifier identity (the backprop-trace engine that re-ran the math), the producer identity (the foreign framework), the differential tolerance bounds, and optional provenance/skip-basis/signature-digest sub-blocks for Rules 14/15/16.

SourceFramework.description: Identifies the framework that produced the math (PyTorch / JAX / TensorFlow / etc.). Modeled on SARIF tool.driver + OpenTelemetry resource.service.name precedent: producer identity at the root, not buried in metadata.

name.description: Closed enum of recognized producer frameworks. 'backprop_trace_engine' is reserved for engine-authored receipts that opt into the source_framework block for cross-reference (typically only used when re-deriving a foreign trace).

version.description: Semver string of the producer framework. Free-form to accommodate non-semver versions (e.g. 'nightly-20260517').

information_uri.description: Optional URI to the framework's home page or release notes. SARIF tool.driver.informationUri analogue. (No JSON-Schema format=uri constraint — Ajv strict mode rejects unknown formats; URI well-formedness is the caller's responsibility.)

extractor.description: Optional. Identifies the adapter that produced the receipt from the framework's native output. Separating producer (the framework) from extractor (the adapter) follows OpenTelemetry semconv precedent: service.name vs telemetry.sdk.name. Conflating them loses ability to debug 'did PyTorch produce wrong numbers, or did our adapter mis-transcribe them?'.

Attestor.description: Identifies the producer (who computed the math) and verifier (the backprop-trace engine that re-ran it), the differential tolerance bounds, and optional provenance / skip-basis / signature-digest sub-blocks. Modeled on in-toto v1 Statement (subject vs predicate vs predicateType) + SLSA Provenance v1.0 builder.id pattern + Sigstore fail-closed discipline.

computed_by.description: Who produced the math. For observer-mode receipts this is the foreign framework. computed_by.kind is the trust class.

verified_by.description: Who re-ran the math as differential witness. For observer-mode receipts this is the backprop-trace engine. computed_by !== verified_by is the load-bearing invariant: a receipt where the producer also self-witnesses is structurally identical to producer-mode and should declare authoring_state accordingly.

differential_tolerance.description: Hybrid tolerance applied to Rule 14 (engine-recompute differential). Looser than engine-authored receipt tolerance because foreign FP precision drifts across CUDA kernels, JIT compilers, vector instructions. Each receipt declares its own tolerance so the verifier knows what claim is being made.

import_provenance.description: OPTIONAL. Provenance bookkeeping for the import event: where the sidecar came from and when it was ingested. SARIF §3.22 conversion block precedent — when a tool transforms native output, the conversion record MUST exist.

source_format.description: Canonical name + version of the input sidecar format. v0.6 ships framework-trace.v0.1.0; future input formats register here.

source_hash.description: SHA-256 hex of the input sidecar bytes (the exact bytes bp import consumed). 'sha256:<hex>' format.

import_timestamp.description: RFC 3339 timestamp at which the importer produced this receipt. (No JSON-Schema format=date-time constraint — Ajv strict mode rejects unknown formats; timestamp well-formedness is the caller's responsibility.)

skip_basis.description: REQUIRED at the reconciler boundary (Rule 15) when fixture_status.verification_state === 'engine_recompute_skipped_with_basis'. Closed enum naming the reason recompute was deliberately not run. Operator must name the basis on the record — silent skipping is rejected (Leroy's verified-vs-trusted discipline applied).

signed_subject_digest.description: OPTIONAL. SHA-256 hex of the canonical-byte hash of the receipt (computed by hashReceipt with this attestor.signed_subject_digest field stripped before hashing). Rule 16 is GATED on this field's presence: when present, the reconciler recomputes the digest and asserts it matches. Catches SolarWinds-style 'signed-but-substituted' attacks where a valid signature is bound to mutated bytes. Signature *validity* (cosign verification of the signature itself) is out of scope for the reconciler — that's a CI-side concern. Rule 16 only checks digest-binding integrity. 'sha256:<hex>' format.

bundle_root_digest.description: OPTIONAL. v0.8+. SHA-256 hex of the canonical-byte digest binding all receipts in a multi-step observer-mode bundle. Rule 17 is GATED on this field's presence: when present, ALL receipts in the same trace MUST carry the same value, AND that value MUST equal sha256 of the canonical concatenation of every receipt's bytes (each receipt re-emitted with its own bundle_root_digest field stripped first, then JSONL-concatenated). Rule 17 catches BUNDLE INTEGRITY failures — accidental splice, post-binding mutation, inconsistent bundle roots — when the digest was not recomputed after the change. Rule 17 is NOT a producer-authenticity check: an attacker who controls all receipt bytes and recomputes the bundle digest passes Rule 17 trivially. For producer-identity binding, combine bundle_root_digest with signed_subject_digest (Rule 16), an external signature, or an out-of-band attestation. Rule 17 is a tamper-evidence layer, not an authentication layer. 'sha256:<hex>' format.

AttestorIdentity.description: Identity of an actor in the attestation chain. Closed enum 'kind' for trust-relevant classification; free-form 'identity' for the actual identity string (URN, framework@version, etc.).
